# Healing Word

- **Level**: 1
- **School**: Evocation
- **Casting Time**: 1 bonus action
- **Range**: 60 feet
- **Components**: V
- **Duration**: Instantaneous
- **Classes**: Bard, Cleric, Druid

## Description

A creature of your choice that you can see within range regains hit
points equal to 1d4 + your spellcasting ability modifier.  This spell
has no effect on undead or constructs.

## At Higher Levels

When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher,
the healing increases by 1d4 for each slot level above 1st.

## Tactical Notes

- **Bonus action economy**: Healing Word is the strongest
  emergency-recovery spell in 5e because it costs only a bonus
  action.  Cast it to bring a downed ally back to 1 HP so they stop
  making death saves — even 1d4+3 healing is enough for that.
- **Ranged heal**: 60 feet beats Cure Wounds' touch range.  You can
  rescue a frontline fighter without ending your turn in melee.
- **Healing output is small**: 1d4 + modifier averages ~5 HP at
  low levels.  Not a substitute for Cure Wounds when out of combat
  and you have unlimited time.
- **Verbal-only component**: Cannot be cast while Silenced.  Most
  tables also rule that it is audible at a distance — stealth
  healing is not possible with this spell.

## Cross-References

- See `cure-wounds.md` for the action-cost touch heal that pairs with
  Healing Word in the same turn: Cure Wounds (action) + Healing Word
  (bonus action) restores the most HP possible in a single round.
